projects.name: Prineville Data Center
projects.notes: Meta's first owned-and-operated data center; campus has expanded multiple times since launch.
projects.source_title: Meta Data Centers — Prineville

at_a_glance.community_grants: Community Action Grants — annual local giving program
at_a_glance.education: STEM programs partnered with Crook County schools

projects.name: Newton County Data Center (Stanton Springs)
projects.notes: Drew local concern after large groundwater withdrawals were reported by local news outlets.
projects.source_title: Meta Data Centers — Newton

at_a_glance.water: Groundwater concerns — NYT documented neighbor well failures Jul 2025

projects.name: Richland Parish Data Center
projects.notes: Announced as Meta's largest data center campus; expected to drive significant new electricity demand on Entergy's Louisiana grid. May 9 2026: Meta quietly acquired an additional ~1,400 acres adjacent to the original 2,250-acre Hyperion site for Phase 2 expansion (purchase from George B. Franklin & Sons); cumulative footprint now ~3,650 acres. Entergy's Apr 30 Q1 disclosure attributes ~$15B of its $57B 4-year capital plan to the Meta deal; LPSC-approved order includes $2B in customer-benefit commitments ($140M energy efficiency over 20 years; $60M Power-to-Care bill assistance match).
projects.source_title: Meta — Investing $10 billion in Richland Parish, Louisiana (Dec 2024)

at_a_glance.infrastructure: Major site expansion under construction in Richland Parish

projects.name: The Dalles Data Center
projects.notes: Google's first dedicated data center campus. Water usage volumes were disclosed only after a multi-year public-records lawsuit.
projects.source_title: Google Data Centers — The Dalles

projects.name: Council Bluffs Data Center
projects.notes: One of Google's largest U.S. campuses; cumulative investment exceeds $5B across multiple expansions.
projects.source_title: Google Data Centers — Council Bluffs

at_a_glance.community_grants: City-wide WiFi service funded; mayor praise on record

projects.name: Mesa Data Center
projects.notes: Sited in a high-water-stress region; Google has highlighted air-cooling and reclaimed-water investments.
projects.source_title: Google Data Centers — Mesa

at_a_glance.engagement: Vice Mayor Duff publicly dissented on water rights tradeoffs

projects.name: Quincy Datacenter Campus
projects.notes: Microsoft's flagship cloud campus; powered largely by Columbia River hydropower.
projects.source_title: Microsoft Datacenters — Quincy

at_a_glance.energy: 95+ diesel generators across MS Quincy complex
at_a_glance.engagement: PCHB ordered annual generator emissions testing post-NYT coverage

projects.name: Mount Pleasant Datacenter Campus
projects.notes: Microsoft's first Wisconsin data center, branded 'Fairwater.' Per Satya Nadella's April 16 2026 statement the site is 'going live ahead of schedule,' combining hundreds of thousands of NVIDIA GB200 GPUs into a single seamless cluster. Mount Pleasant Village Board approved 15 additional buildings across two new lots on May 11 2026 (~$13B+ incremental taxable value). 375 FTEs hired as of May 8 2026 per Bowen Wallace at the RCEDC Impact 2026 event.
projects.source_title: Microsoft expands Wisconsin investment to $3.3B (May 2024)

at_a_glance.infrastructure: Hundreds of thousands of NVIDIA GB200s in single AI cluster

projects.name: Goodyear Datacenter Campus
projects.notes: Located in metro Phoenix; community concerns have focused on aquifer use as Maricopa County faces ongoing water-supply scrutiny.
projects.source_title: Microsoft Datacenters — West region

at_a_glance.water: Switched to air cooling due to AZ groundwater constraints

projects.name: AWS US-East-1 (Northern Virginia)
projects.notes: AWS's largest U.S. presence and the heart of 'Data Center Alley'; concentration has driven both substantial tax revenue and persistent local debate over land use, transmission corridors, and noise.
projects.source_title: AWS Global Infrastructure — US East (N. Virginia)

at_a_glance.tax_revenue: Anchor of Loudoun's data-center tax base (US-East-1)
at_a_glance.engagement: Noise + zoning pushback; Loudoun tightened DC standards post-2024

projects.name: Project Rainier (New Carlisle)
projects.notes: AWS-built compute cluster anchored by hundreds of thousands of AWS Trainium2 chips; primary tenant is Anthropic for frontier model training.
projects.source_title: AWS to invest $11B in Indiana for new data center campus (Oct 2024)

at_a_glance.water: Residential well failures reported locally; IDEM wetland citations

projects.name: Cumberland Valley Data Center Campus
projects.notes: Two-campus build on land adjoining the Susquehanna Nuclear Plant in Salem Township, Luzerne County PA, structured around a behind-the-meter PPA with Talen Energy. (Project name 'Cumberland Valley' is a Talen-era branding; the actual location is Luzerne County, not Cumberland County PA.)
projects.source_title: AWS — $20B Pennsylvania investment (June 2024)

at_a_glance.energy: 1,920 MW behind-the-meter PPA with Talen (Susquehanna nuclear)
at_a_glance.engagement: FERC rejected amended ISA Nov 2024 — regulatory uncertainty

projects.name: Stargate Abilene Campus
projects.notes: First Stargate site, developed by Crusoe and operated as Oracle Cloud Infrastructure capacity for OpenAI. Initial buildings underway in 2024–2025.
projects.source_title: OpenAI — Announcing the Stargate Project (Jan 2025)

at_a_glance.energy: 1.2 GW campus — OpenAI workload, Crusoe operates
at_a_glance.infrastructure: Downstream housing crisis + local electrician shortage

projects.name: OCI Stargate Capacity (Abilene)
projects.notes: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure capacity tenancy at the Stargate Abilene campus, contracted to OpenAI as the primary workload.
projects.source_title: Oracle and OpenAI extend cloud partnership (2024)

projects.name: Colossus Supercomputer (Memphis)
projects.notes: Built in roughly four months in a former Electrolux plant in southwest Memphis; on-site gas turbines drew air-quality complaints from neighbouring residents and the local NAACP chapter.
projects.source_title: xAI — Colossus

at_a_glance.energy: 27→46 unpermitted gas turbines; NAACP/SELC Clean Air Act lawsuit pending
at_a_glance.water: $80M water-recycling plant paused Apr 2026 then recommitted

projects.name: Wonder Valley Utah Data Center Campus
projects.notes: Joint venture between O'Leary Digital Limited (Kevin O'Leary, chairman; Paul Palandjian, CEO) and West GenCo LLC, announced Feb 17, 2026. Located in the Golden Spike District of Box Elder County. On May 4, 2026 the Box Elder County Commission approved the Stratos Project Area (~40,000 acres of private land + ~1,200 acres of military/state parcels) at Phase 1 ~3 GW scaling to a long-term 7.5–9 GW buildout — without a completed environmental review. Construction now expected to begin 2028. Self-generating power via on-site gas turbines ('Shadow Grid' model, Ruby Pipeline natural gas). Also referred to as the 'Stratos Project.'
projects.source_title: O'Leary Digital Forms Joint Venture to Advance 7.5 GW Wonder Valley Utah Campus; Alberta Permitting Underway

at_a_glance.energy: Phase 1 ~3 GW scaling to 7.5-9 GW; on-site gas via Ruby Pipeline

projects.name: QTS Cedar Rapids Data Center Campus
projects.notes: 612-acre campus at Big Cedar Industrial Center (6200 76th Ave SW), southwest Cedar Rapids near the airport. Up to seven data center buildings totaling ~1.4M sq ft; first phase targeting late-2028 commissioning. Reported 1.05 GW total planned capacity. Lead utility partner: Alliant Energy. Canonical reference for QTS's 'ratepayer protection pledge' commitment — QTS pledged to pay 100% of its own energy costs and Alliant CEO Lisa Barton publicly committed to a five-year, zero-percent rate freeze for existing Iowa customers. Investment grew from $750M (Feb 2025) to $1.75B (April 2026). ~8,500 construction workers on-site daily as of April 2026.
projects.source_title: City of Cedar Rapids, Alliant Energy, and QTS Collaborate on New Data Center Campus in Cedar Rapids

at_a_glance.energy: Ratepayer Protection Pledge — QTS pays 100% of grid upgrade costs
at_a_glance.engagement: Alliant CEO support; Iowa advocate skepticism on record

projects.name: QTS Richmond Technology Park Data Center 5 (RIC5)
projects.notes: Located in Henrico County's White Oak Technology Park, near the I-64/I-295 interchange. QTS acquired 622 acres for ~$137M in 2024; up to 13 buildings. RIC5 became the FIRST data-center project ever to receive FAST-41 federal-permitting coverage (designated April 2, 2026 by the Federal Permitting Improvement Steering Council). Lead federal agency: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers. Power served by Dominion Energy via two new 230-kV lines and an expansion of the White Oak substation (Virginia SCC approved March 2026). Specific MW capacity for RIC5 not disclosed.
projects.source_title: First Data Center Project Gains Permitting Council's FAST-41 Coverage

at_a_glance.infrastructure: 622 ac — first-ever FAST-41 federal permitting designation for a DC

projects.name: QTS Dane County Data Center Campus
projects.notes: Spans DeForest and Vienna in Dane County, Wisconsin. QTS announced a $50 million community commitment alongside the project — scholarships and workforce partnerships with Madison Area Technical College, research partnerships with the University of Wisconsin, contribution to the Wisconsin Frontier Technology Platform, nonprofit and first-responder support. 750 MW renewable-energy procurement agreement with Alliant Energy and 40 acres of wetland habitat preservation. Water-free cooling design.
projects.source_title: QTS Advances Plans for State-of-the-Art Data Center Campus and Announces $50 Million Community Commitment for Dane County

at_a_glance.community_grants: $50M community fund (Madison Area Technical College + UW)
at_a_glance.engagement: WI DOA Jan 2026 found annexation not in public interest; 4,000+ opposed

projects.name: QTS Manassas Data Center Campus
projects.notes: 100+ acre campus at 10680 University Blvd, Prince William County, adjacent to existing QTS Manassas data centers. QTS is also the named developer behind the controversial Prince William Digital Gateway (PWDG) — a ~2,139-acre rezoning approved by Prince William supervisors November 2022 after a 27-hour debate. PWDG faces ongoing litigation; the Virginia Court of Appeals ruled in favor of plaintiffs (Oak Valley HOA, American Battlefield Trust); QTS filed an 11th-hour appeal.
projects.source_title: Prince William Digital Gateway - QTS Data Centers

projects.name: Google Van Buren Township Data Center
projects.notes: 1 GW hyperscale campus on a 282-acre site in Wayne County (north of I-94, between Haggerty Road and I-275), originally advanced as 'Project Cannoli'. Power served by 2.7 GW of new DTE solar, storage and demand response. Service start targeted December 2027, full capacity December 2028. Google announced a $10M Energy Impact Fund for Michigan energy affordability. Project is in a contested case before the Michigan Public Service Commission with a September 10, 2026 decision target. Local opposition includes a 1,500+ signature petition.
projects.source_title: Scaling clean energy and reliability in Michigan

ratepayer.summary: Google and DTE filed contracts with the Michigan Public Service Commission confirming Google finances all 2.7 GW of new power supply needed, ensuring no costs associated with the Van Buren Township campus shift to existing ratepayers.

new_generation.note: Google agreed to finance all 2.7 GW of new DTE power through a contract filed with the Michigan PSC.

delivery_infra.note: DTE/Google contract covers all transmission and distribution upgrade costs; existing customer bills are unaffected.

separate_rate.note: Separate rate structure filed with the Michigan Public Service Commission; contested case hearing scheduled for September 2026.

local_jobs.note: Google committed a $10 million Energy Impact Fund for Michigan affordability, weatherization, and workforce development programs.

grid_resilience.note: Project integrates with DTE and MISO grid reliability plans; battery storage is included in the 2.7 GW build.

projects.name: Microsoft Cheyenne Datacenter Campus (2026 Expansion)
projects.notes: April 14, 2026 announcement of intent to purchase ~3,200 acres south of Cheyenne (200 acres in Bison Business Park on Wapiti Trail east of S Greeley Highway, plus 3,000 acres in Southeast Cheyenne) to triple Microsoft's existing Cheyenne footprint. Microsoft has been operating in Cheyenne since 2012 and was the No. 1 taxpayer in the city in 2025. Cumulative community investments include $68M in off-site infrastructure improvements and 56 community projects with 28 local organizations since 2018. On ~May 28, 2026 Microsoft added two more parcels — a 385-acre tract near the 3,200-acre site (close to Laramie County Community College) and ~35 acres near North Range Business Park — bringing the cumulative announced footprint to ~3,620 acres.
projects.source_title: Microsoft announces intent to expand datacenter operations in Cheyenne

at_a_glance.tax_revenue: $11M+ to Laramie County tax base 2025; no local abatements
at_a_glance.energy: Black Hills LPCS tariff: MS pays its own grid upgrades directly

ratepayer.summary: Covered by Microsoft's national pledge signature and its broader 'pay our way' commitment; the Cheyenne expansion details tax base, off-site infrastructure, and a no-abatements stance, but no Cheyenne-specific power-cost pledge was captured.

new_generation.note: Covered by Microsoft's national pledge; no site-specific generation agreement captured for this campus.

delivery_infra.note: Covered by Microsoft's national pledge; no site-specific grid upgrade agreement captured for this site.

separate_rate.note: Covered by Microsoft's national pledge; no separate rate filing or utility agreement captured for this site.

local_jobs.note: Covered by Microsoft's national pledge; no site-specific local hiring or workforce commitment captured.

grid_resilience.note: Covered by Microsoft's national pledge; no site-specific grid coordination or backup commitment captured.

projects.name: OpenAI Stargate Lordstown
projects.notes: Announced as one of five new Stargate sites by OpenAI/Oracle/SoftBank in 2026. Built on the former GM/Lordstown Motors/Foxconn plant that SoftBank acquired from Foxconn for $375M in August 2025. SoftBank plans to invest up to $3B at Lordstown to manufacture equipment for OpenAI data centers; the Lordstown facility itself is on track to be operational in 2026. Combined with Foxconn-Stargate manufacturing, the plant could employ as many as 1,700 workers. Aggregate Stargate capacity announced is ~7 GW with >25,000 onsite jobs across all sites.
projects.source_title: OpenAI, Oracle, and SoftBank expand Stargate with five new AI data center sites

at_a_glance.infrastructure: Brownfield redevelopment of legacy auto-manufacturing site

ratepayer.summary: Covered by OpenAI's Stargate Community pledge ('pay our own way on energy'); no Lordstown-specific utility agreement or named-executive commitment for this site has been published.

new_generation.note: Covered by OpenAI's national pledge; no site-specific generation agreement captured for this campus.

delivery_infra.note: Covered by OpenAI's national pledge; no site-specific grid upgrade agreement captured for this site.

separate_rate.note: Covered by OpenAI's national pledge; no separate rate filing or utility agreement captured for this site.

local_jobs.note: Covered by OpenAI's national pledge; no site-specific local hiring or workforce commitment captured.

grid_resilience.note: Covered by OpenAI's national pledge; no site-specific grid coordination or backup commitment captured.

projects.name: Stargate Abilene AI Factory Campus (Crusoe)
projects.notes: 1.2 GW build-out across 8 buildings on Lancium's Clean Campus. Same physical site as openai-abilene-tx and oracle-abilene-tx (Crusoe is developer/operator; OpenAI is workload owner; Oracle delivers cloud infrastructure). Phase includes 900 MW on-site power plant + MV BESS. Crusoe projects up to 32% of City of Abilene and 25% of Taylor County FY2025 budgeted property tax revenue once fully operational, ~$1B direct + indirect economic impact over 20 years from first two buildings. 5,600 daily construction workers on-site as of August 2025; local hiring prioritized. Closed-loop non-evaporative liquid cooling.
projects.source_title: Crusoe — An inside look at the Abilene AI data center (Aug 2025)

at_a_glance.energy: 1.2 GW — Crusoe operates Stargate Abilene for OpenAI
at_a_glance.engagement: Lochmiller publicly framing project as data-center 'blueprint'

projects.name: CoreWeave Hammond Data Center (Digital Crossroads)
projects.notes: 180 MW AI data center, 450,000 sq ft on a 25-acre parcel at 301 Digital Crossroads Drive. Development agreement approved 8-0 by Hammond City Council on June 9, 2025; described by the city as the largest economic-development project in Hammond's history. Annual Community Impact Payment of up to $4 million to the City of Hammond, directed by the city toward the Hammond College Bound scholarship program (two decades of full program funding implied) and city-wide infrastructure and quality-of-life initiatives. Contingent on finalization of a NIPSCO power agreement and execution of the lease with Decennial Group.
projects.source_title: City of Hammond — CoreWeave Development Agreement (Jun 9, 2025)

at_a_glance.education: $4M/yr for 20 yrs ($80M) to Hammond College Bound scholarships
at_a_glance.engagement: City Council 8-0 approval on Community Impact Payment

projects.name: Polaris Forge 1 Campus (Applied Digital lease)
projects.notes: AI HPC campus leased from Applied Digital (additional 150 MW finalized in 2025 on top of original lease). Designed to harness North Dakota renewable power and cool-climate free cooling; CoreWeave projects ~$2.7B in lifecycle TCO savings over 30 years. Primary community-impact framing on this site is environmental (sustainable cooling + renewables) rather than community grants.
projects.source_title: Applied Digital Finalizes Additional 150MW Lease with CoreWeave in North Dakota

projects.name: Tulsa Data Center (Project Anthem)
projects.notes: 340-acre site at Fair Oaks Innovation Park in east Tulsa. Meta's first Oklahoma data center / 28th US site. ~1,000 peak construction workers, ~100 operational FTEs. $62.5M PILOT over 25 years (85% property-tax abatement). $25M+ committed for local road/water infrastructure; $3M in community grants.
projects.source_title: Meta Data Centers — Hello, Tulsa! (Apr 2026)

ratepayer.summary: Meta said it pays the full costs of the Tulsa data center's energy, water, and wastewater service so that consumers are not paying its costs.

new_generation.note: Meta stated it pays the full costs of the Tulsa campus's energy use so consumer rates are unaffected.

delivery_infra.note: Meta VP Gary Demasi confirmed Meta pays 100% of all infrastructure and road improvements for the Tulsa site.

separate_rate.note: Covered by Meta's national pledge; no separate rate filing or utility agreement captured for this site.

local_jobs.note: Meta partnered with Tulsa Tech and Tulsa Community College to train up to 200 students/year for trade jobs at the campus.

grid_resilience.note: Covered by Meta's national pledge; no site-specific grid coordination or backup commitment captured.

projects.name: Beaver Dam Data Center
projects.notes: 700,000 sq ft AI-optimized campus in Alliant Energy's Beaver Dam Commerce Park. Up to 220 MW per Alliant service contract; Meta is underwriting ~$200M in energy infrastructure (substations, network upgrades). Wisconsin PSC approved rates in April 2026 with criticism that future ratepayers may be exposed. Dry-cooled; commits to restoring 570 acres of adjacent wetland/prairie habitat.
projects.source_title: Meta Data Centers — Hello, Beaver Dam! (Nov 2025)

projects.name: LaGrange Data Center (Project Pegasus)
projects.notes: 420-acre site at 411 Pegasus Parkway, Troup County (former Jindal Films plant). Google's second Georgia data center. ~400 MW estimated capacity. Google commits to paying 100% of power + infrastructure costs. $1M Groundswell SOUL energy-efficiency grant; Trust for Public Land partnership for Chattahoochee River habitat restoration.
projects.source_title: Atlanta News First — Google announces new data center in LaGrange (Apr 21 2026)

at_a_glance.water: Trust for Public Land — Chattahoochee restoration partnership

ratepayer.summary: Covered by Google's national pledge signature; the LaGrange announcement focused on being a good neighbor and local investment, with no site-specific ratepayer commitment captured.

new_generation.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no site-specific generation agreement captured for this campus.

delivery_infra.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no site-specific grid upgrade agreement captured for this site.

separate_rate.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no separate rate filing or utility agreement captured for this site.

local_jobs.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no site-specific local hiring or workforce commitment captured.

grid_resilience.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no site-specific grid coordination or backup commitment captured.

projects.name: Stillwater Data Center
projects.notes: 400-acre site, part of Google's $9B 2026-2027 Oklahoma expansion (Pryor + Stillwater + Muskogee). Phased AI-focused build; ~300,000 sq ft per data center. OG&E partnership; Google pays 100% of grid-connection costs. Partnerships with University of Oklahoma and Oklahoma State for AI workforce training; $1.5M regenerative-agriculture water-conservation program statewide.
projects.source_title: Google — New $9 billion investment in Oklahoma (Apr 29 2026)

at_a_glance.water: Park View Estates HOA sediment-runoff lawsuit pending
at_a_glance.engagement: HOA litigation underway over construction runoff

ratepayer.summary: Covered by Google's national pledge signature; the Oklahoma announcement highlighted long-standing presence and innovation rather than a site-specific ratepayer commitment.

new_generation.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no site-specific generation agreement captured for this campus.

delivery_infra.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no site-specific grid upgrade agreement captured for this site.

separate_rate.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no separate rate filing or utility agreement captured for this site.

local_jobs.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no site-specific local hiring or workforce commitment captured.

grid_resilience.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no site-specific grid coordination or backup commitment captured.

projects.name: Person County Datacenter (Woodsdale)
projects.notes: 1,350-acre former Person County Mega Park near Roxboro NC, purchased Nov 2024 for $26.85M. First North Carolina test of Microsoft's January 2026 Building Community-First AI Infrastructure pledge: pay own electricity costs, water-positive cooling, IT training, tax-base contribution. Permitting started April 2026; community NDA controversy resolved March 2026 with Microsoft's national commitment to stop signing community-blinding NDAs on new sites.
projects.source_title: Microsoft Local — Person County datacenter project

ratepayer.summary: Covered by Microsoft's national 'pay our own way' commitment and Duke Energy's standard data center contract language; no Person County-specific ratepayer filing or named executive quote tied to this site has been captured.

new_generation.note: Covered by Microsoft's national pledge; no site-specific generation agreement captured for this campus.

delivery_infra.note: Covered by Microsoft's national pledge; no site-specific grid upgrade agreement captured for this site.

separate_rate.note: Covered by Microsoft's national pledge; no separate rate filing or utility agreement captured for this site.

local_jobs.note: Covered by Microsoft's national pledge; no site-specific local hiring or workforce commitment captured.

grid_resilience.note: Covered by Microsoft's national pledge; no site-specific grid coordination or backup commitment captured.

projects.name: AWS Madison County (Canton) Data Centers
projects.notes: Mississippi's largest economic-development project. $11B incremental commitment in April 2026 brings total to $21B for Madison County buildout (1,000+ jobs). Entergy's $300M 'Superpower' grid investment + 616 MW of contracted renewable generation support the site. Canton facility commits to 100% recycled-wastewater cooling by 2027.
projects.source_title: Amazon — $25 billion Mississippi data center expansion (Apr 9 2026)

at_a_glance.energy: 240 tons/yr NOx from 300+ diesel backups; resident health concerns

projects.name: AWS Hinds County (Clinton) Data Center
projects.notes: Retrofit of the former Delphi/Aptiv manufacturing plant in Clinton, MS into an AWS data center; $1B and 100 jobs announced April 9 2026 as part of AWS's broader $25B Mississippi commitment. Reuses existing industrial infrastructure.
projects.source_title: Amazon — $25 billion Mississippi data center expansion (Apr 9 2026)

ratepayer.summary: Amazon's April 2026 Mississippi-wide announcement covers the Clinton/Hinds County campus: Amazon pays 100% of all new energy infrastructure costs under its Entergy Mississippi agreement, with no costs passed through to existing ratepayers.

new_generation.note: Amazon and Entergy Mississippi confirmed Amazon pays 100% of all new energy infrastructure costs for the Clinton campus.

delivery_infra.note: Amazon's April 2026 Mississippi announcement covers all grid upgrade costs with zero pass-through to ratepayers.

separate_rate.note: Covered by Amazon's national pledge; no separate rate filing or utility agreement captured for this site.

local_jobs.note: Covered by Amazon's national pledge; no site-specific local hiring or workforce commitment captured.

grid_resilience.note: Covered by Amazon's national pledge; no site-specific grid coordination or backup commitment captured.

projects.name: Stargate Michigan (Saline Township)
projects.notes: 1.4 GW Stargate site: three 550,000 sq-ft buildings on 1,000 acres (575 rezoned + 475 in Bridgewater Twp). Closed-loop air cooling; LEED-targeted. Related Digital developer; Oracle 15-year tenant; OpenAI workload. Blackstone equity, PIMCO debt; $16B financing closed Apr 24 2026. Township board originally voted 4-1 to reject rezoning Oct 2025; Related sued and reached settlement before construction began Nov 2025. Estimated $8M/yr in school tax revenue, $1.6M/yr direct township revenue, $14M direct community benefits (fire department, community fund, farmland preservation).
projects.source_title: Blackstone — Related Digital $16B Oracle/Stargate Michigan financing close (Apr 24 2026)

projects.name: Stargate Doña Ana (Project Jupiter)
projects.notes: 1,400-acre, four-building campus in Doña Ana County, NM. Bloom Energy fuel-cell-powered as of Apr 27 2026 announcement (~92% NOx reduction vs combined-cycle gas, negligible water use). Up to ~2.4 GW. Closed-loop non-evaporative cooling. 4,000 construction + 1,500 ongoing jobs. Workforce-training partnership with Doña Ana Community College + Oracle Academy at NMSU. $600M+ projected Gross Revenue Tax payments to state + county. OpenAI is the workload tenant.
projects.source_title: Oracle — Project Jupiter powered by Bloom Energy fuel cells (Apr 27 2026)

at_a_glance.tax_revenue: $360M direct payments to Doña Ana County
at_a_glance.energy: Bloom Energy fuel cells; ~92% NOx reduction vs gas turbines
at_a_glance.water: Closed-loop non-evaporative cooling; $50M for local water systems

projects.name: Eagle Mountain Data Center (SLC1)
projects.notes: 193-acre, three-building QTS campus (~580K sq ft each). Topping-out ceremony April 18, 2026 with ~2,000 peak construction workers; ~100 permanent operational FTEs. Closed-loop zero-water cooling. QTS publicly commits no impact on local utility rates per its Ratepayer Protection Pledge.
projects.source_title: Utah Business — QTS Eagle Mountain topping out (Apr 20 2026)

at_a_glance.energy: Ratepayer Protection Pledge: no impact on local utility rates

projects.name: ATL2 East (Fayetteville Expansion)
projects.notes: 313-acre expansion site west of Veterans Parkway, adjacent to QTS's existing Fayetteville campus. 2 million sq ft data center planned; targeted completion 2029. Broader Fayetteville campus envelope (615 acres) targets 800-1,000 permanent jobs. QTS filed DRI application with GA Dept of Community Affairs in 2026.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — QTS files to expand Fayetteville campus

projects.name: York County (Clover) Data Center
projects.notes: $8B QTS campus on Hands Mill Hwy / Campbell Rd. ~9 buildings approved with future expansion under evaluation. ~200 permanent jobs / ~1,000 peak construction. $40M in projected tax revenue earmarked for local schools, $16M for York County. May 12, 2026 community Q&A at Oakridge Middle School amid growing residential opposition.
projects.source_title: Post and Courier — QTS meets with York County residents about $8B plant (May 2026)

projects.name: East Windsor Data Center Expansion
projects.notes: Proposed 272,000 sq ft, two-story, 77-foot data center on 52-acre existing site at 159 Princeton-Hightstown Rd. Existing facility has >70 MW with on-site substation. April 27, 2026 planning board meeting postponed due to incomplete public testimony — a signal of NJ's more constrained data-center permitting environment.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — QTS East Windsor expansion vote postponed (Apr 2026)

projects.name: Sweetwater Data Center
projects.notes: 200 MW initial facility in Nolan County, TX on a $7B build-out path. ~40 permanent + ~2,000 peak construction jobs. Nolan County approved a 10-year tax abatement in April 2026; ~$3M annual property-tax payments after abatement plus ~$2.2M annual charitable funding for city use. Project subject to water-use restrictions.
projects.source_title: KTXS — Nolan County approves $7B Crusoe tax abatement (Apr 2026)

projects.name: Project Jade (Switchgrass Industrial Park)
projects.notes: 1.8 GW initial scaling toward 10 GW campus in Laramie County WY. Gas-fired generation via Tallgrass BFC Power / Cheyenne Power Hub ($7B power-infrastructure tranche). ~5,000 peak construction. Q2 2027 target for first-building energization.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — Crusoe 1.8 GW Cheyenne campus + power plant

projects.name: Springfield Data Center
projects.notes: Smaller-scale Crusoe deployment, 20 permanent jobs at $1.5M annual payroll per state announcement. Crusoe declined comment to local outlets at time of state disclosure.
projects.source_title: Springfield News-Sun — Crusoe Springfield data center, 20 jobs

projects.name: Kenilworth NEST Campus
projects.notes: Northeast Science and Technology Center (NEST): 392,600 sq ft data center on former Merck campus (acquired for $322M July 2025). 143 permanent jobs at >120% county-median salary. $250M five-year tax credit ($50M/yr) under NJ EDA's Next New Jersey Program — AI — the FIRST award under that program. Scaling to up to 250 MW at full build; operational target early 2027.
projects.source_title: Real Estate NJ — CoreWeave begins $1.8B Kenilworth data center, first NJ EDA AI award

projects.name: Lancaster Data Center
projects.notes: Initial 100 MW IT capacity scaling to 300 MW. 75 permanent jobs at launch (scaling to 175); 600+ construction jobs. Co-developed by Chirisa Technology Parks + Machine Investment Group. ~$200M power-grid investment commitment. Lancaster Mayor working on community benefits agreement.
projects.source_title: CoreWeave — Building Pennsylvania into the Mid-Atlantic AI Hub

projects.name: Project Maize (Michigan City)
projects.notes: Google confirmed as buyer of 'Project Maize' on a former Federal Mogul brownfield at 402 Royal Road. Construction was already underway when Google was identified. Google commits to pay for power and infrastructure upgrades. Local opposition cites water draw, sound, and diesel backup-generator emissions.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — Google takes over Project Maize data center campus in Michigan City, Indiana

ratepayer.summary: Google committed to paying 100% of the cost of power for the Michigan City site and any infrastructure needs driven by its operations.

new_generation.note: Google committed to paying 100% of the power cost for the Michigan City site and any infrastructure needs driven by its operations.

delivery_infra.note: Google's power cost commitment covers generation; no separate grid upgrade contract specific to Michigan City is on record.

separate_rate.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no separate rate filing or utility agreement captured for this site.

local_jobs.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no site-specific local hiring or workforce commitment captured.

grid_resilience.note: No grid coordination or backup generation information has been captured for the Michigan City site.

projects.name: Lima Data Center (Project BOSC)
projects.notes: Google's third Ohio data center, on a 170-acre site in American Township (Allen County). Up to three buildings; construction begins 2026 with 2030 target. Commitments include $50M for local roundabouts (Bluelick Rd / N Cole St; SR-115 / N Cole St), $13.6M for local water infrastructure, and $250K/yr to Elida Local Schools as a 15-year tax-abatement offset.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — Google confirmed as company behind $500m data center in Lima, Ohio

ratepayer.summary: Covered by Google's national pledge signature; Lima commitments cover roads, water infrastructure, and school funding rather than a site-specific power-cost / ratepayer pledge.

projects.name: Scioto County Data Center
projects.notes: 500,000 sq ft data center confirmed after Scioto County commission awarded a 75% / 15-year tax abatement; PILOT is $500K/yr base plus per-square-foot compensation once complete. Heavy local pushback on Ohio River wetlands impact, stormwater, and truck traffic.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — Google confirmed as company behind 500,000 sq ft data center in Scioto County, Ohio

ratepayer.summary: Google Area Operations Manager Timothy Chadwick stated publicly at a May 2026 community information fair that Google will pay 100% of its electricity costs and all AEP infrastructure for the Scioto County campus.

new_generation.note: Google Area Manager Timothy Chadwick stated publicly that Google will pay 100% of electricity costs for the Scioto County campus.

delivery_infra.note: Google's Chadwick commitment covers power costs; no separate AEP grid upgrade contract is documented for Franklin Furnace.

separate_rate.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no separate rate filing or utility agreement captured for this site.

local_jobs.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no site-specific local hiring or workforce commitment captured.

grid_resilience.note: No grid coordination or backup generation information has been captured for the Scioto County site.

projects.name: Linn County (Duane Arnold) Campus
projects.notes: Up to six data-center buildings near the Duane Arnold Energy Center. NextEra-Google PPA to restart the Duane Arnold nuclear plant; Google takes >90% of plant output. Community-betterment payments of $1M/building/yr with a 2% annual escalator. Estimated water draw at full buildout: 12-14M gallons/day.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — Google looks to build up to six data centers near nuclear power site in Linn County, Iowa

ratepayer.summary: Covered by Google's national pledge signature; Linn County terms center on community betterment payments rather than a site-specific ratepayer commitment.

projects.name: Stargate Milam County (Freebird)
projects.notes: Phase 1 disclosed in a TDLR filing: single-story 548,950 sq ft data center with four data halls, completion target Oct 15. SB Energy (SoftBank subsidiary) is developing toward an eventual 1.2 GW facility. Phase 1 'topped out' per former Stargate lead Peter Hoeschele. Phase-1 capex $470M is a fraction of the eventual ~$5B+ full build cost.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — OpenAI Stargate's Milam, Texas, 'Freebird' data center to span 548,950 sq ft in first phase

projects.name: Lebanon Data Center
projects.notes: Broke ground Feb 11, 2026; Meta's second Indiana site (after New Carlisle is AWS, not Meta — Lebanon is Meta's first solo IN campus). $10B+ AI-optimized site, online late 2027 / early 2028. ~4,000 peak construction + 300 operational FTEs. Meta committed $120M for local water infrastructure and $45M for public roads.
projects.source_title: Meta — Lebanon Indiana data center marks milestone AI investment (Feb 11 2026)

ratepayer.summary: Meta's own newsroom and the City of Lebanon both confirmed Meta pays the full costs for energy at the Lebanon campus, with Boone REMC and Wabash Valley Power Alliance covering service at no added cost to existing customers.

new_generation.note: Meta's newsroom and the City of Lebanon both confirmed Meta pays the full costs of the campus's energy use.

delivery_infra.note: Meta confirmed it pays 100% of all infrastructure improvements required to serve the Lebanon campus.

separate_rate.note: Covered by Meta's national pledge; no separate rate filing or utility agreement captured for this site.

local_jobs.note: Meta committed to local workforce partnerships in Boone County, with training programs supporting the 500+ permanent jobs announced.

grid_resilience.note: Covered by Meta's national pledge; no site-specific grid coordination or backup commitment captured.

projects.name: El Paso Data Center
projects.notes: Originally announced 2024 at $1.5B; expanded March 26 2026 to a $10B / 1 GW AI-optimized campus. Liquid-cooled closed-loop; Meta to restore 200% of water consumed. ~366 MW backup via 813 small gas-fired generators (controversial — emits CO/NOx). Online 2028.
projects.source_title: Meta Data Centers — Big things are happening, El Paso! (Mar 26 2026)

at_a_glance.energy: 366 MW backup via 813 gas generators (CO/NOx concerns)
at_a_glance.water: Closed-loop liquid cooling; 200% water restoration pledge

projects.name: Chesterfield Data Center (Project Peanut)
projects.notes: Part of Google's $9B Virginia investment (announced March 17 2026). 307-acre, 855,846 sq ft campus at 2700 Bermuda Hundred Road; three buildings (~285K sq ft each). Two additional county sites (Moseley 880 ac, Westchester Commons 350 ac) under evaluation but not yet committed.
projects.source_title: Virginia Business — Google announces Chesterfield data center as part of $9B investment (Mar 17 2026)

ratepayer.summary: Virginia's SCC-approved GS-5 rate class requires Dominion Energy data center customers to cover their own grid upgrade costs; no Project Peanut-specific Google commitment letter or site-level filing has been published.

projects.name: Putnam County Data Center
projects.notes: Multi-billion-dollar campus announced March 27 2026 under West Virginia's High Impact Data Center Project framework. Google land purchase approved by state. Google covers 100% of electricity costs and all electrical/water/sewer upgrades; 120% water replenishment commitment.
projects.source_title: WV Governor's Office — Google Data Center Project Putnam County announcement (Mar 27 2026)

ratepayer.summary: Covered by Google's national pledge signature; the Putnam County announcement is early-stage (land purchase) with no site-specific ratepayer commitment captured yet.

projects.name: Stargate Wisconsin (Lighthouse)
projects.notes: Stargate Lighthouse: four-building, 672-acre Ozaukee County campus announced Oct 22 2025. 902 MW IT load (1.3 GW total electrical) — 'close to a gigawatt.' ~4,000 union construction + 700 Oracle/OpenAI on-site + 330 Vantage operations FTEs. Vantage Data Centers is the developer; OpenAI is the workload tenant.
projects.source_title: Construction Dive — OpenAI/Oracle $15B Stargate site in Wisconsin (Oct 22 2025)

projects.name: AWS Falls Township Data Center Campus
projects.notes: Bucks County PA, NorthPoint Development site. Second of two PA campuses announced jointly with Salem Township (Luzerne Co) under AWS's $20B Pennsylvania commitment.
projects.source_title: Falls Township — Amazon to Open Data Center at NorthPoint Development in Falls

projects.name: AWS Richmond County (Hamlet-Rockingham) Campus
projects.notes: 800-acre, $10B build at Energy Way Industrial Park. 20 buildings ~200-225K sq ft each. Largest single capital project in NC history. Duke Smith Energy Complex's 2,200 MW gas-fired adjacent capacity supports the load.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — Amazon breaks ground on $10bn Richmond County NC complex

projects.name: AWS Caddo Parish Campus
projects.notes: AWS's first north Louisiana site; broke ground March 2026. Companion to Bossier Parish campus; together ~7M sq ft. $400M LA water-infrastructure commitment. 540 on-site / 1,700 indirect jobs across both LA parishes.
projects.source_title: Louisiana Economic Development — Amazon $12B Louisiana data center campuses (Feb 23 2026)

ratepayer.summary: Amazon and SWEPCO President Brett Mattison jointly confirmed at the February 2026 announcement that Amazon pays 100% of all project-specific infrastructure costs, with zero pass-through to residential or business customers.

new_generation.note: Amazon and SWEPCO President Brett Mattison jointly confirmed Amazon pays 100% of all project-specific infrastructure costs at the Caddo campus.

delivery_infra.note: Amazon's joint SWEPCO announcement covers all delivery infrastructure with zero pass-through to residential or business customers.

grid_resilience.note: SWEPCO coordination is noted in the joint announcement; no formal grid resilience or backup generation agreement has been published for Caddo Parish.

projects.name: AWS Bossier Parish Campus
projects.notes: Companion site to Caddo Parish; jointly announced under AWS's $12B LA commitment Feb 23 2026.
projects.source_title: KSLA — AWS data center spanning Bossier & Caddo parishes confirmed

ratepayer.summary: Part of the same joint Caddo/Bossier announcement: Amazon and SWEPCO confirmed Amazon pays 100% of all new energy infrastructure costs for both Louisiana campuses, with no costs passed through to residential or business customers.

new_generation.note: Same joint Caddo/Bossier announcement: Amazon pays 100% of all new energy infrastructure costs across both Louisiana campuses.

delivery_infra.note: Amazon's joint SWEPCO announcement covers all grid upgrade costs at the Bossier Parish campus with zero ratepayer pass-through.

projects.name: AWS Warren County Data Center
projects.notes: Part of April 9 2026 $12B central Mississippi expansion announcement, alongside Madison County ($11B) and Hinds County / Clinton ($1B). Brings AWS Mississippi total commitment to $25B / 2,000+ jobs.
projects.source_title: Amazon Newsroom — $25 billion Mississippi data centers (Apr 9 2026)

ratepayer.summary: Amazon's April 2026 Mississippi announcement explicitly states it pays 100% of all grid infrastructure and upgrade costs for its Mississippi campuses, including Warren County/Vicksburg, under its Entergy Mississippi agreement.

new_generation.note: Amazon's April 2026 Mississippi announcement explicitly states it pays 100% of all energy infrastructure costs at the Vicksburg campus.

delivery_infra.note: Amazon's Mississippi announcement covers all grid upgrade costs for Vicksburg with zero pass-through to existing utility customers.

projects.name: Salem Township Campus (PA)
projects.notes: 1,700-acre QTS / Blackstone land acquisition in Luzerne County PA closed March 2026; 96 landowners participated in $500M+ sale. Could host 12-17 data centers per local reporting. Construction begins fall 2026. Distinct from but adjacent to the AWS Salem Township (Cumberland Valley) campus already tracked.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — QTS acquires 1,700 acres in Pennsylvania

projects.name: Southaven (Colossus 3 / MACROHARD)
projects.notes: DeSoto County MS facility codenamed MACROHARD / Colossus 3, third xAI building across the TN-MS border (complementing the Memphis Colossus 1 + 2 sites). Powered by 41 natural-gas turbines at a former Duke Energy site — permit issued April 29 2026 by Mississippi regulators amid the NAACP Clean Air Act lawsuit. Elon Musk pledged $20B in state investment.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — xAI confirms new Mississippi data center, Musk pledges $20bn

ratepayer.summary: Covered by xAI's national pledge signature; the Southaven permitting announcements emphasized speed, capacity, and jobs, with no site-specific ratepayer commitment captured.

new_generation.note: Covered by xAI's national pledge; no site-specific generation agreement captured for this campus.

projects.name: Stargate Frontier (Shackelford County)
projects.notes: Vantage Data Centers 'Frontier' campus — 10 single-story buildings totaling ~3.7M sq ft across 1,200 acres near Albany / Moran TX. Air- and liquid-cooled at >250kW/rack densities. One of three new Oracle-partnership sites from the September 2025 five-site Stargate expansion. First building H2 2026.
projects.source_title: Vantage Data Centers — Frontier $25B mega-campus in Texas

projects.name: New Albany Data Center Campus
projects.notes: One of three Microsoft Central Ohio campuses (with Heath and Hebron) totaling ~$1B initial investment. 245,000 sq ft site on ~200 acres at the Licking-Franklin county boundary.
projects.source_title: One Columbus — Microsoft Data Center Investment in Columbus Region

projects.name: Heath Data Center Campus
projects.notes: Five data-center buildings planned, completion early 2028. AMES Construction general contractor; mass grading Feb–Nov 2026. Site off Thornwood Drive in Licking County.
projects.source_title: Microsoft Local — Heath datacenter construction updates

projects.name: Hebron Data Center Campus
projects.notes: Six data center buildings planned. Construction began February 2026. Site off High Street, ~30 miles east of Columbus in Licking County.
projects.source_title: NBC4 — Microsoft begins construction on Hebron data center

projects.name: Union City Campus (East US 3 anchor)
projects.notes: Three buildings ~612,000 sq ft each (2.1M sq ft total) at 4810 Stonewall Tell Road, Fulton County. Fulton County approved a $75M tax break. Phase 1 online by 2026; additional phases through 2032. Anchors Microsoft's 'East US 3' Azure region (Fulton + Douglas Counties).
projects.source_title: Microsoft Azure — Next US datacenter region in Georgia's Fulton and Douglas Counties

projects.name: West Des Moines Campus (5 operational + 6th in construction)
projects.notes: Microsoft's largest US data-center deployment (per company statement re Project Osmium). Includes Projects Mountain, Alluvion, Osmium; 6th site 'Ruthenium' on Veterans Pkwy at Woodland Ave (Madison County). ~70M gallons/year water from West Des Moines Water Works in 2024. Spans Dallas/Polk/Warren/Madison counties.
projects.source_title: Business Record — West Des Moines approves agreement with Microsoft for 6th data center

projects.name: West Memphis Campus (Project Pyramid)
projects.notes: Five-building campus on 1,178 acres south of Bollinger Road in Crittenden County. Google's first Arkansas data center; broke ground October 2025. 600 MW solar via Entergy. Google covers full energy costs. $25M Crittenden County community fund. Construction 18-24 months.
projects.source_title: Google Blog — American Innovation: Arkansas (West Memphis Project Pyramid)

projects.name: Port of Little Rock Data Center
projects.notes: 1.43M sq ft across 5 industrial buildings + 2 office buildings + a substation, off Zueber Road in Sweet Home (Pulaski County). Reveal Jan 2026; site plan in progress. Active community opposition centered on water draw and ratepayer impact.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — Google behind $1bn data center in Little Rock, Arkansas

ratepayer.summary: Entergy Arkansas VP Ventrell Thompson told the Little Rock City Board that Google is required to pay its full infrastructure costs; however, as of June 2026 no final rate agreement for the Port of Little Rock site has been signed or published.

new_generation.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no site-specific generation agreement captured for this campus.

projects.name: AWS Boardman / Columbia River Land Acquisition
projects.notes: 1,300 acres on the Oregon side of the Columbia River (Morrow County) acquired 2026; potential for up to 20 data-center buildings. Adjacent to the AWS Hermiston regional office opened March 2026. Formal project approval pending.
projects.source_title: GeekWire — Amazon buys 1,300 acres near Columbia River for potential data center

ratepayer.summary: Oregon's POWER Act (HB 3546, 2025) statutorily requires new large energy users to pay 100% of distribution infrastructure costs; Amazon's 1,300-acre Morrow County site is pre-development with no site-specific commitment yet filed.

new_generation.note: Covered by Amazon's national pledge; no site-specific generation agreement captured for this campus.

delivery_infra.note: Covered by Amazon's national pledge; no site-specific grid upgrade agreement captured for this site.

separate_rate.note: Oregon's POWER Act (HB 3546, 2025) requires new large energy users to fund their own power supply; this commitment is met by statute for all Oregon campuses.

local_jobs.note: Covered by Amazon's national pledge; no site-specific local hiring or workforce commitment captured.

grid_resilience.note: Covered by Amazon's national pledge; no site-specific grid coordination or backup commitment captured.

projects.name: Project Blue Hole (Blakely Campus)
projects.notes: 12M sq ft mega-campus in southwest Georgia near the Alabama border (Early County). ~9 buildings planned, closed-loop cooling, construction July 2026 → first phase Sept 2027; full build-out targeted Q4 2035. DRI filing March 2026.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — QTS targets two Georgia campuses (Blakely + Fayetteville)

projects.name: DFW2 Campus (Wilmer)
projects.notes: 303 Mason Rd at the Wilmer/Lancaster border (Dallas County). DC2 470,000 sq ft (June 2026 completion); DC3 560,000 sq ft (Jan 2027 completion). Combined DC1+DC2 critical load 75.2 MW. $300M (DC2) + $350M (DC3) capex.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — QTS files to expand Dallas DFW2 campus

projects.name: Google New Florence Data Center
projects.notes: $15B campus (developer codename 'Project Spade', developed by Related Digital) on a ~934-acre site in the southeast quadrant of the I-70 / Missouri-19 interchange near New Florence, Montgomery County — announced May 20, 2026 as the largest single investment in Missouri history. Two buildings, ~800,000 sq ft, closed-loop air-cooled, targeting ~1.2 GW. Construction expected to begin 2026 (~2,000 construction jobs over 2–3 years; ~200 long-term operational roles). Sits next to a separately developed AWS campus. Google also operates a Kansas City, MO data center referenced in the same announcement.
projects.source_title: Google Deepens Missouri Roots with New Data Center, Energy, and Community Investments (PR Newswire)

at_a_glance.energy: 1.2 GW target; 1 GW+ new MO capacity contracted
at_a_glance.water: Air-cooled; water limited to domestic use

ratepayer.summary: Google's New Florence announcement states the company pays for 100% of the power it uses and covers infrastructure costs its operations drive — a site-specific echo of the pledge.

new_generation.note: Google's New Florence announcement states the company pays 100% of the power and covers all infrastructure costs for this campus.

delivery_infra.note: Google's commitment covers all grid infrastructure costs, ensuring no upgrade expenses shift to existing Ameren Missouri customers.

separate_rate.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no separate rate filing or utility agreement captured for this site.

local_jobs.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no site-specific local hiring or workforce commitment captured.

grid_resilience.note: Covered by Google's national pledge; no site-specific grid coordination or backup commitment captured.

projects.name: Clinton Data Center Campus (proposed)
projects.notes: Proposed QTS campus on ~3,400 acres zoned M5 Industrial, west of Valley Oaks Golf Course and east of South 34th Street — ten ~50,000 sq ft buildings, ~$1.5B. Proposed May 2026; not yet approved. A packed May 26 city council meeting drew resident concern over water, electricity, health, and transparency, and the council agreed to place a 120-day data-center moratorium on its June 3, 2026 agenda. No verbatim first-party QTS quote has been published yet (coverage paraphrases QTS), so no Claim is recorded for this site.
projects.source_title: Clinton City Council considering data center moratorium (Clinton Herald)

projects.name: Project Sail (Coweta Campus)
projects.notes: 832-acre, 9-building, 4.34M sq ft data-center campus ~45 miles south of Atlanta. Sited deliberately near Georgia Power's Plant Yates; load to be served via the DOE-loan-funded Plant Wansley + new Ashley Park-Wansley 500 kV transmission line (the same line at issue in the May 2026 eminent-domain disputes against Fayette and Coweta County homeowners). Coweta County rezoning approved 3-2 on 2026-04-10; 17-plaintiff residents lawsuit (Citizens for Rural Coweta) filed 2026-05-05 in Coweta County Superior Court. Tenant not yet publicly disclosed. Up to 9 million gallons/day projected water draw. Prologis estimates $100M tax revenue over 10 years.
projects.source_title: AJC — A new group steps in to develop $17B Project Sail data center near Atlanta

at_a_glance.energy: 900 MW; sited at Plant Yates / Plant Wansley corridor

projects.name: AWS Madison County Campus (Ridgeland Expansion)
projects.notes: Part of Amazon's $25B Mississippi commitment announced April 9, 2026. Expansion of Amazon's existing Madison County operations cluster in Ridgeland, served by Entergy Mississippi. Amazon pays 100% of all grid infrastructure and upgrade costs under the same Entergy Mississippi agreement covering all Amazon's Mississippi campuses.
projects.source_title: Amazon — $25 billion Mississippi data centers announcement (Apr 2026)

ratepayer.summary: Amazon's April 2026 Mississippi announcement explicitly covers all new campuses including Ridgeland/Madison County: pays 100% of grid infrastructure costs via its Entergy Mississippi agreement.

new_generation.note: Amazon's April 2026 Mississippi announcement covers all Ridgeland/Madison campuses; Amazon pays 100% of energy infrastructure costs.

delivery_infra.note: Amazon's Mississippi-wide announcement covers all grid upgrade costs for Ridgeland with zero ratepayer pass-through.

separate_rate.note: Covered by Amazon's national pledge; no separate rate filing or utility agreement captured for this site.

local_jobs.note: Covered by Amazon's national pledge; no site-specific local hiring or workforce commitment captured.

grid_resilience.note: Covered by Amazon's national pledge; no site-specific grid coordination or backup commitment captured.

projects.name: AWS Clinton County Campus (Wilmington)
projects.notes: AWS acquired ~590 acres in Jefferson Township, Clinton County in August 2025. Proposed $4B campus on 471 acres off US-68. As of May 2026, twice tabled by Clinton County planning commission due to community opposition and unresolved approval questions. Legal action filed by neighbors. Port authority negotiating with Wilmington City Schools on a community benefit deal.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — AWS Wilmington Ohio project tabled (2026)

projects.name: Google Pine Island Data Center
projects.notes: Announced February 24, 2026. 480-acre campus in Pine Island Township, Goodhue County, served by Xcel Energy. Google committed to finance 1,900 MW of new clean energy resources for the Minnesota grid (solar, wind, battery storage). $25M community investment fund over 20 years, benefiting Pine Island schools. Google covers 100% of new grid infrastructure costs. ~500 construction trades during build phase.
projects.source_title: Google Blog — Pine Island, Minnesota data center announcement (Feb 2026)

projects.name: Google Hermantown Data Center
projects.notes: Confirmed as Google March 3, 2026 (project initially codename 'Project Birch'). 403-acre campus adjacent to Minnesota Power's Arrowhead substation, St. Louis County. Partnership with Minnesota Power to enable 700 MW of new clean energy (300 MW wind + 400 MW battery storage) — no cost increases to existing customers. $5M energy affordability fund for low-income customers. As of May 2026, Hermantown City Council tabled a key zoning vote (May 5, 2026) amid ongoing permitting and utility agreement negotiations.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — Google confirms 403-acre Hermantown, Minnesota campus

projects.name: Google Wilbarger County Data Center
projects.notes: Part of Google's $40B Texas investment package announced February 24, 2026. Air-cooled campus in Wilbarger County, Texas Panhandle. Co-located with AES clean energy generation under a 20-year power purchase agreement. On ERCOT grid.
projects.source_title: Google Blog — Wilbarger County, Texas data center announcement (Feb 2026)

projects.name: Google Armstrong County Data Center
projects.notes: Part of Google's $40B Texas investment package. 1,300-acre site in Armstrong County, Texas Panhandle (near Claude, TX). Wind energy-powered via ERCOT. Closed-loop cooling for minimal water use. Crusoe Energy assisting with site preparation; construction underway as of early 2026.
projects.source_title: ABC7 Amarillo — Google Armstrong County data center announcement

projects.name: Google Haskell County Data Center
projects.notes: Part of Google's $40B Texas investment package. Two data center campuses in Haskell County, West Texas. One campus co-located with a solar plus battery storage project. On ERCOT grid. Construction underway as of early 2026.
projects.source_title: KTXS — Google $40B data center investment in Haskell County, Texas

projects.name: Microsoft La Porte Data Center (2nd Campus)
projects.notes: Second Microsoft campus in La Porte County, adjacent to the original 2024 campus (489 acres, 6 buildings, $1B). April 14, 2026: La Porte voted to annex ~1,000 acres in Pleasant Township. May 18, 2026: City council approved annexation and rezoning for 11 additional data center buildings on the expanded footprint. Original La Porte campus announced June 2024; this second campus is a distinct post-pledge expansion.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — Microsoft expands La Porte, Indiana data center plans (2026)

ratepayer.summary: Covered by Microsoft's national pledge signature; the May 2026 La Porte expansion approval focused on annexation and zoning with no site-specific ratepayer commitment captured for this campus.

projects.name: Stargate Michigan
projects.notes: 250-acre Stargate campus in Washtenaw County. Three 550,000-sq-ft buildings powered by DTE Energy at 1,400 MW. Site selected by Related Digital / OpenAI in October 2025; formal Stargate groundbreaking June 1, 2026. Project includes a $10 million investment in the Saline Recreation Center.
projects.source_title: OpenAI — Stargate Michigan

at_a_glance.energy: 1,400 MW DTE-served; company pays its own way

ratepayer.summary: Covered by OpenAI's Stargate 'pay our own way on electricity' commitment and the national pledge signature; no site-specific rate contract filed with Michigan regulators has been captured for the Saline Township campus.

separate_rate.note: No Michigan PSC filing or utility rate agreement has been captured for the Saline Township campus.

local_jobs.note: A $10 million investment in the Saline Recreation Center was announced at the June 2026 groundbreaking, with 2,500+ union construction jobs committed.

grid_resilience.note: Covered by OpenAI's national pledge; no site-specific grid coordination agreement has been captured for the Saline campus.

projects.name: AWS Wheatfield Data Center Campus
projects.notes: Nine-building data center campus in Jasper County, Indiana. Reported by Data Center Dynamics June 3, 2026. No investment, MW, or jobs figures disclosed at announcement.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — Amazon plans nine-building data center campus in Wheatfield, Indiana

ratepayer.summary: Covered by Amazon's national Ratepayer Protection Pledge signature; no site-specific commitment or utility agreement has been filed for the Wheatfield campus.

new_generation.note: Covered by Amazon's national pledge; no site-specific generation agreement captured for this campus.

delivery_infra.note: Covered by Amazon's national pledge; no site-specific grid upgrade agreement captured for this site.

separate_rate.note: Covered by Amazon's national pledge; no separate rate filing or utility agreement captured for this site.

local_jobs.note: Covered by Amazon's national pledge; no site-specific local hiring or workforce commitment captured.

grid_resilience.note: Covered by Amazon's national pledge; no site-specific grid coordination or backup commitment captured.

projects.name: Montgomery Data Center Campus
projects.notes: 715,000 sq ft initial building off I-65, across from Hyundai's Alabama plant. Investment expanded from $800M (May 2024 announcement) to $1.5B (September 2025) when Meta added two more buildings, targeting 1.3M sq ft total. AI-optimized campus powered by 100% Alabama renewable energy (227 MW PPA). 100+ operational jobs; 1,000+ peak construction workers. First phase targeted for commissioning late 2026.
projects.source_title: Governor Ivey Announces Meta Plans to Build $800 Million Data Center in Montgomery (May 2024)

projects.name: Google Botetourt County Data Center Campus
projects.notes: Three-building campus in Botetourt County (Roanoke Valley), VA. Approximately 300,000 sq ft per building; site grading expected before end of 2026, first building targeted for completion around 2028. County projected to receive $30M+ in annual tax revenue. Google secured a PPA for 80 MW Rocky Forge Wind (Virginia's first utility-scale wind project). Up to 8 million gallons per day water use anticipated. Google contributed $4M to county infrastructure initiatives at time of announcement.
projects.source_title: Google Data Center — Botetourt County, Virginia Government

projects.name: Google Sand Springs Data Center (Project Spring)
projects.notes: 827-acre, three-phase campus near the Tulsa County / Creek County border, ~8 miles west of downtown Tulsa. City of Sand Springs approved rezoning in February 2026; construction targeted for 2027. Up to ~$3B total across phases. Google pays 100% of new grid infrastructure costs (dedicated substation + transmission upgrades) so no costs shift to existing utility customers. 25-year PILOT tax agreement: $1.3M–$3.9M per facility per year. Separate site from google-stillwater-ok.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — Google gets greenlight for data center near Tulsa, Oklahoma

projects.name: Google Kansas City Data Center (Project Mica)
projects.notes: 500-acre, five-building, 1.56M sq ft campus in Clay County (KC Northland, ~12701 N Arrowhead Thruway). Port KC authorized $10B in taxable revenue bonds in July 2025. 700 MW planned capacity. Construction confirmed February 2026. Google committed $1.75M in upfront workforce funding ($1.5M to Smithville School District, $250K to Northland Career Center). Uses a Large Load Power Tariff so Google covers 100% of energy costs and no grid upgrade costs shift to existing ratepayers. Separate from google-new-florence-mo (New Florence / Montgomery County campus).
projects.source_title: Axios KC — Google 'Project Mica' data center campus in KC Northland (Feb 2026)

projects.name: Google Meitner Energy Center (Gray County / Texas Panhandle)
projects.notes: Co-located data center and 1 GW+ renewable energy campus spanning Gray County and Roberts County in the Texas Panhandle. Google acquired Intersect Power (March 2026) to develop the on-site wind, solar, battery storage, and firming gas generation. Air-cooled design — zero water consumed in operations. Caprock Workforce Hub: 800-acre residential campus in Wheeler County housing ~3,500 construction workers. $10M Texas Water Impact Fund commitment for community water infrastructure. Groundbreaking announced June 4, 2026.
projects.source_title: Google Blog — Meitner Energy Center (June 4, 2026)

ratepayer.summary: Announced June 2026 after the White House Ratepayer Protection Pledge; Google's co-owned on-site generation model is consistent with the pledge, but no site-specific first-party commitment text has been captured yet.

projects.name: Microsoft Castroville Datacenter Campus (SAT 82)
projects.notes: 195,670 sq ft, five-module colocation building (SAT 82) in Medina County, west of San Antonio. Part of Microsoft's South Central Cloud Region campus network (SAT 80, 81, 82 across ~1,451 total acres in Medina County). Construction targeted to begin August 2026; completion targeted June 2028. Contributes to Microsoft's 500+ employee presence across existing San Antonio-area campuses.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — Microsoft plans $400M data center in Castroville, Texas

projects.name: Microsoft Douglasville Datacenter Campus (East US 3)
projects.notes: Douglas County component of Microsoft's East US 3 cloud region (announced 2023, $1.8B committed across Fulton and Douglas counties). Four-building campus (~1M sq ft) on 160 acres targeting 216 MW total electrical capacity (144 MW critical IT load). Phase I targeting completion 2026; East US 3 cloud region launch expected 2027. Distinct from microsoft-union-city-ga (Fulton County anchor campus in Union City).
projects.source_title: Microsoft Azure Blog — East US 3 datacenter region in Fulton and Douglas Counties, Georgia

projects.name: AWS Caroline County Data Center Campus (Mattermeade)
projects.notes: 1,143-acre campus straddling Caroline and Spotsylvania counties at I-95 / Mudd Tavern Rd (Ruther Glen, VA). Army Corps of Engineers permit filed 2023; construction started October 2025. Eleven two-story data center buildings targeting 770 MW total capacity by 2027. Part of AWS's $35B Virginia commitment by 2040, extending the Northern Virginia data center corridor south toward Richmond. Proximate to a proposed Duke Energy 2.24 GW gas generation complex.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — Amazon requests permit for 11-building data center campus in Caroline County, Virginia

projects.name: QTS Van Wert Data Center Campus
projects.notes: 902-acre campus in Van Wert County, northwest Ohio (bordered by Mendon Rd, US-224, and US-30). Up to seven buildings; 500 MW total capacity. AEP partnership for energy supply. First groundbreaking targeted Q4 2026; first building operational Q1 2029. Closed-loop cooling with zero water consumption. QTS funds 100% of energy infrastructure at no cost to existing ratepayers. Community betterment fund covering education, workforce, public safety, and community amenities. $100K donated to Vantage Career Center at announcement. Projected $200M in local tax revenue over 20 years; 1,500+ construction workers and 200 permanent QTS positions.
projects.source_title: Data Center Dynamics — QTS announces $10B Van Wert, Ohio data center campus (May 2026)

projects.name: Stargate Wisconsin (Lighthouse) — OpenAI
projects.notes: OpenAI's primary workload tenant at the Stargate Lighthouse campus in Ozaukee County. Oracle operates the cloud infrastructure; Vantage Data Centers is the developer. Four-building, 672-acre campus with ~902 MW IT load (1.3 GW total electrical). Groundbreaking December 2025. Parallel record to oracle-port-washington-wi — same physical campus, OpenAI workload perspective. Follows the same dual-record pattern as oracle-abilene-tx / openai-abilene-tx.
projects.source_title: OpenAI — Announcing the Stargate Project
